page: Google Cloud Platform h3-standard-88 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 88 dedicated physical cores with one thread per core, operating at a base frequency of 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 352 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX-tile, AMX-int8), alongside hardware-accelerated cryptography (AES, VAES, SHA_NI). In benchmark testing, the h3-standard-88 demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations. However, single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench remains in the average tier.

The memory subsystem delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth for both small cached and large uncached blocks, though the PassMark memory score is rated as average. The server achieves top-tier throughput in static web serving and Redis SET operations, alongside strong performance in database operations and software compilation. For machine learning workloads, the processor's AMX capabilities support strong prompt processing speeds for large language models, though text generation speeds fall into the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the h3-standard-88 offers high resource density and dedicated compute resources without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. This makes it a viable option for compute-heavy parallel workloads that do not require local NVMe drives. Recommended workloads include multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform h3-standard-88 is an x86_64 server configured with 88 dedicated physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 352 GB of RAM. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. It features advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. In benchmarks, the server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and static web serving throughput, while single-core execution speeds remain average. The h3-standard-88 provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, making it a cost-efficient option for highly parallelized workloads that benefit from dedicated compute resources without local storage overhead. It is suited for multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based large language model prompt processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform h3-standard-88 is an x86_64 server with 88 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 352 GB of RAM. It delivers top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform h3-standard-88 features 88 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 352 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
tagline: A dedicated 88-core x86_64 server delivering top-tier multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 88 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores with 352 GB of system memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth in benchmarks
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, reducing resource overhead
Suited for multi-threaded software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
